revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ceb:Kabe Shima (pulo sa Hapon, Saga-ken)]][[co:commons:Category:Kabeshima]][[fr:Île de Kabe]][[ja:加部島]][[simple:Kabe Island]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ceb:Kabe Shima (pulo sa Hapon, Saga-ken)]][[co:commons:Category:Kabeshima]][[fr:Île de Kabe]][[ja:加部島]][[simple:Kabe Island]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ceb:Kabe Shima (pulo sa Hapon, Saga-ken)]][[co:commons:Category:Kabeshima]][[fr:Île de Kabe]][[ja:加部島]][[simple:Kabe Island]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ceb:Kabe Shima (pulo sa Hapon, Saga-ken)]][[fr:Île de Kabe]][[ja:加部島]][[simple:Kabe Island]]
{{Infobox islands|image=Kabeshima Island from Sakigata, Yobuko, Karatsu 1.JPG|島名=加部島|image=[[File:Kabeshima Island from Sakigata, Yobuko, Karatsu 1.JPG|250px]]|image説明=呼子台場みなとプラザの北側から望む加部島|緯度度=33|緯度分=33|緯度秒=20|経度度=129|経度分=53|経度秒=0|面積=2.68|周囲=8|標高=112.2|最高峰=天童岳|海域=[[日本海]]（[[玄界灘]]）|国={{JPN}} [[佐賀県]][[唐津市]]|地図2=Japan Saga|OSMズーム=11|area_km2=2.95|timezone1={{ill|UTC+09:00|en|UTC+09:00|qid=Q7041|lt=UTC+09:00|1=UTC+09:00}}|location={{ill|Sea of Japan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan|comment=no wikipedias work|qq=}} ({{ill|Genkai Sea|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|comment=no wikipedias work|qq=}})|coordinates={{coordinates|33|33|20|N|129|53|0|E}}|coastline_km=8}}
Kabe Island, known in Japanese as {{Nihongo|Kabe-shima|加部島}} is an {{ill|Island|en|Island|qid=Q23442|lt=Island|1=Island}} located in the {{ill|Genkai Sea|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Sea|4=fr|5=Mer de Genkai|comment=no wikipedias work|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}},<ref name="gn1860924">[{{Geonameslink|gnid=1860924|name=kabe%20shima}} Kabe Shima] sa [{{Geonamesabout}} Geonames.org (cc-by)]; post updated 2013-08-07; database download sa 2017-02-07</ref> approximately 500 meters off the coast of [[:ja:呼子港|Yobuko Port]] in {{ill|Yobuko,_Saga|en|Yobuko,_Saga|qid=Q8054238|lt=Yobuko Town|1=Yobuko, Saga}}, {{ill|Karatsu,_Saga|en|Karatsu,_Saga|qid=Q738275|lt=Karatsu City|1=Karatsu, Saga}}, in the northern part of [[:ja:東松浦半島|the Higashi Matsuura Peninsula]] . It is connected to Tonoura in Yobuko Town on the mainland by [[:ja:呼子大橋|the Yobuko Bridge]] . It belongs to Karatsu City {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|qid=Q160420|lt=, Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}}, and its district name is Kabeshima, Yobuko Town, Karatsu City.
Kabe Island is located in Saga Prefecture in southwestern Japan, the island is approximately 900 km west of [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]], the country's capital. It covers 3.0 square kilometers.
It is an important island to the local economy. <ref name="gn1860924" /> <ref name="vp">{{Cite web|date=2015-06-21|title=Viewfinder Panoramas Digital elevation Model|url={{Viewfinderlink}}|format=}}</ref> <ref name="nasa" /> <ref name="nasarain" />
The land of Kabe Island is flat. {{Efn|Calculation from height data (DEM 3") from Viewfinder Panoramas.<ref name = "vp"/> The full algorithm is available [https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Projekt_DotNetWikiBot_Framework/Lsjbot/Make-Geonames here].|group=saysay}} The highest point of the island is 92 meters above sea level. It covers 2.9 km from north to south and 1.9 km from east to west. {{Efn|Calculation from height data (DEM 3") from Viewfinder Panoramas.<ref name = "vp"/> The full algorithm is available [https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Projekt_DotNetWikiBot_Framework/Lsjbot/Make-Geonames here].|group=saysay}}
The climate is temperate . The average temperature is 15 °C . The hottest month is August, with an average temperature of 24 °C, and the coldest January, with an average temperature of 8 °C. <ref name="nasa">{{Cite web|title=NASA Earth Observations Data Set Index|url=http://neo.sci.gsfc.nasa.gov/dataset_index.php|access-date=30 Enero 2016|publisher=NASA}}</ref> The average rainfall is 2,403 millimeters per year. The wettest month is August, with 484 millimeters of rain, and the driest is January, with 43 millimeters. <ref name="nasarain">{{Cite web|title=NASA Earth Observations: Rainfall (1 month - TRMM)|url=http://neo.sci.gsfc.nasa.gov/view.php?datasetId=TRMM_3B43M|access-date=30 Enero 2016|publisher=NASA/Tropical Rainfall Monitoring Mission}}</ref>
{{Location map|Japan|width=300|float=right|caption=Location|label=Kabe Shima|position=right|background=white|lat_deg=33.55601|lon_deg=129.88143}}
== Overview == The area of the island is approximately 2.68km<sup>2</sup>
The population was 464 as of November 2022, having declined from 583 in 2010 and 762 in 1995.
It is located right in front of Yobuko Port and serves as a natural breakwater protecting the port from the rough waves of the Genkai Sea. The watershed lies to the far south of the island, while the highest peak, Mount Tendo, is located in the southwest.
On the other hand, the northern side of the island is a plateau with an expanse of flat land. The geology is {{ill|Basalt|en|Basalt|qid=Q43338|lt=basalt|1=Basalt}}, and the northern coast consists of {{ill|Columnar jointing|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Columnar jointing|comment=no wikipedias work|lt=columnar jointed|qq=}} sea cliffs . <ref name="kot">「[https://kotobank.jp/word/加部島-46394 加部島] 世界大百科事典,日本大百科全書」、{{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|qid=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank|1=Kotobank}}、2015th year 9月5日閲覧</ref>
There are two settlements: Kabeshima and Katashima. Along with fishing, such as {{ill|Japanese_amberjack|en|Japanese_amberjack|qid=Q1202973|lt=yellowtail|1=Japanese amberjack}} farming and {{ill|squid|en|squid|qid=Q81900|lt=squid|1=Squid}} fishing, agriculture is also thriving, with rice, vegetables, and citrus fruits being cultivated, and beef cattle being raised. Local specialties include {{ill|amanatsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/amanatsu|comment=no wikipedias work|qq=}} (Yobuko amanatsu) and amanatsu jelly made from it. <ref name="kot">「[https://kotobank.jp/word/加部島-46394 加部島] 世界大百科事典,日本大百科全書」、{{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|qid=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank|1=Kotobank}}、2015th year 9月5日閲覧</ref>
It is part of {{ill|Genkai Quasi-National Park|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genkai Quasi-National Park|comment=no wikipedias work|qq=}} . <ref>「[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/web/at-contents/kanko_bunka/shizenkoen/sizenkouen/genkai.html 玄海国定公園]」佐賀県、2015th year 9月5日閲覧</ref>
In 2009, the island's "farmland landscape protected by windbreaks" was selected as [[:ja:にほんの里100選|one of the "100 Best Villages of Japan]] " by the Asahi Shimbun Company and the Forest Culture Association. <ref>「にほんの里100選 [http://www.sato100.com/?page_id=261 86. 加部島]」、朝日新聞社・森林文化協会、2015th year 9月5日閲覧</ref>
The early 6th century {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} "Hyozuka Kofun" located in Suginohara Ranch in the northern part of the island is said to be the tomb of Emperor {{ill|Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|4=fr|comment=no wikipedias work|qq=|qid=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} 's son, Prince Wakatake. Additionally, [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima-jinja,]] which is mentioned in the Matsuura Kojiki and has within its grounds the Sayohime Shrine associated with the legend of {{ill|Matsuura Sayohime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuura Sayohime|comment=no wikipedias work|qq=}}, is said to be the oldest shrine in Saga Prefecture and is also a historic island that received special treatment from the Imperial Court during the time of the Japanese envoys to the Tang Dynasty.
== Traffic == The 727m-long {{ill|8=ja|9=呼子大橋|Yobuko Bridge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Yobuko Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11417065}} connects the southern part of the island with Tonoura in Yobuko Town on the mainland. There is a signpost for the Yobuko Bridge on {{ill|Japan National Route 204|en|Japan National Route 204|qid=Q6156935|lt=National Route 204|1=Japan National Route 204}} in Yobuko Town, Karatsu City.
Showa Bus operates local buses and shared taxis that pass over the Yobuko Bridge and connect the {{ill|8=ja|9=昭和自動車|Yobuko bus stop|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Showa Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q128159}} in downtown Yobuko with Kabeshima. There are bus stops on the island, at the foot of the Yobuko Bridge and on the east side of the island. It takes about 15 minutes from Sugimura, Kabeshima to Yobuko. At Yobuko, it connects with Showa Bus, which runs between Karatsu city center ({{ill|8=ja|9=唐津大手口バスセンター|Karatsu Oteguchi Bus Center|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|1=Q11418624|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11418624}}) and Yobuko.
== Climate ==
{| |{{Climate chart|Kabe Shima|7|9|43|7|10|159|7|12|154|10|16|138|16|19|111|16|19|322|19|20|380|23|25|484|19|21|209|19|21|136|13|17|158|9|13|109}} |} == Tourist attractions ==
* [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima-jinja]] * Sayohime Shrine * Wind View Hill Park * Suginohara Ranch ==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kabeshima Island from Sakigata, Yobuko, Karatsu 1.JPG|Kabeshima Island seen from Sakigata in Yobuko, Karatsu - View 1 File:Kabeshima Island from Sakigata, Yobuko, Karatsu 2.JPG|Kabeshima Island seen from Sakigata in Yobuko, Karatsu - View 2 File:Nihon fūkei senshū, Hizen Kabeshima by Kawase Hasui.jpg|Artistic rendition of Hizen Kabeshima by Kawase Hasui File:Rural landscape of Kabeshima from Kazeno-mieru-oka Park.jpg|Rural landscape of Kabeshima from Kazeno-mieru-oka Park File:Takashima and Kabashima Islands from Sakigata, Yobuko, Karatsu.JPG|Takashima and Kabeshima Islands seen from Sakigata in Yobuko, Karatsu File:Yobuko Port and Kabeshima Island 20140726-1.JPG|Yobuko Port and Kabeshima Island File:Yobuko Town from Kazeno-mieru-oka Park.jpg|Yobuko Town as seen from Kazeno-mieru-oka Park File:田島神社.JPG|View of Tashima Shrine File:田島神社全景.jpg|Full view of Tashima Shrine </gallery>
